reviews.review_msg: Had Denise for CA 414. She seems level-headed and easy to talk to. I know some people who have had her for bigger classes like CA 118, and I can confidently say her teaching is better in seminars. You talk about sustainability a lot. She gives lots of room for improvement throughout the assignments.

1.0 ratings are from lazy kids. Easiest class I had, just sit back and learn about paintings. No textbook just take lecture notes. Easy to memorize the 57 artworks if you listen in class. Gave us long answer test questions in advance so you knew exactly how to prepare. Don't know how you could get less than a B. Not my major but I'd take again fs.
Her teaching style is a bit bland and does ramble onto different topics. But I'm thankful that she gives out study guides to prepare for the exam. Grading was fair and exams are okay.

She either goes on a whole tangent about things she won't test you on, or say one sentence about things she will test you on. She will ask for feedback and when we give her our honest opinion, she gets supper defensive. She'll ask you to memorize 57 artworks and not include 95% of them on the midterm.

The pacing of the class is horrible. She gave us the outline for the midterm a week before the exam and expected us to remember 57 pieces. She asked us for feedback on the midterm before we even got them back. When people would give criticism, she would get super defensive. In front of the class, she would say who scored well on the exam.
for sfu sca students who have a choice, don't do it. it's not worth your time (notably this class is at a weird time of the day), your energy, and spirit. avoid at all costs

gave us the midterm study guide a WEEK before the midterm with 57 ARTWORKS to memorize. respect for giving us the questions to prepare our short/long answers before the midterm but with the lack of engagement during lectures, youre forced teach yourself the content. learned more from the assigned readings which takes an hour than the lectures

if you have the CHOICE to take this class, DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. asked for feedback on the midterm then got defensive and offended by what students had to say about formatting. we were given 57 works to learn on our own (because she does not teach engagingly) to only be tested on, at most 12 of them.
#bringDorothyback

The epitome of has a PhD credential with zero ability to explain concepts or present knowledge in an engaging way. Complete waste of time and extremely passive aggressive. Asks for feedback then gets extremely defensive with any form of criticism.

Dr. Denise is literally an expert in her field she deserves your respect and your listening ears. I've taken two classes with her, she will pour as much effort into you as you do into the course. With my chronic pain she was empathetic and caring. Dr. Denise is generous with her time in office hours and she is genuinely excited by new ideas!

5% of the course is the participation so you basically need to show up. Had the pleasure of having Denise and she really taught me a lot about art history in ways I've never known before. All of the homework is just reading and writing, and being analytical. Lots of discussion happens during lectures as well. Would love to take nother course w her!

Don't take if you don't have to. Astoundingly disorganized & unengaging. Monotone script-reader who relies on the class to talk when she has no points of her own. Unclear-but-demanding assignments & exams, & their instructions seem to change every time she's asked to clarify. Hard marker, even in participation marks - for a class with no tutorials!

Shes very sweet and knowledgeable - Ill give her that much, and Im sure for face to face classes shes much better, but if the course is remote Id decide against this prof. Shes very unorganized, posts assignments late, mixes up due dates, doesnt know how to zoom works (thank god her TA was always there) and the lectures are very monotone.

I walked out of class almost not learning anything. The slides consisted of purely only paintings. As someone in the below said, she relies too much on readings and discussions. She is very monotoned and it is hard to stay focused in the class. Good thing the final was take-home exam due to COVID-19.

Denise is a very nice and intelligent, she genuinely cares for the students. However I wish she gave more information rich lectures as I felt that too much relies on class discussion or readings alone at time. A little scattered at times but a lovely person.
Excellent teacher.  Kind and inspiring

Denise is a really nice person and she wants to see her students succeed, which is nice these days.  However, I feel like I haven't learned anything from her and am relying on what I've learned in other classes to get me by.  She is also really horrible at keeping office hours, so make sure to MAKE her know you are coming, but don't expect much.
Denise is nice, but her teaching style is all over the place. I walked away learning nothing from her class.

Denise is a nice enough person but once she starts teaching all sense of logic and relevance flys out the window. This class may as well been distance ed cause she is completely useless, the only reason I didnt drop this course is cause it's manditory but if you can avoid if AVOID her!
Excellent teacher.  Very kind.  Thoughtful.  She creates a learning environment.
Holy hell, I can't believe that there hasn't been one negative comment for this woman. I couldn't stand her attitude or her teaching style.

She is amazing!!! I highly recomnad her to everyone!!! She knows her stuff and she is very passionate about what she is doing! It's inspiring to be in her class. She really listen to the students and is very supportive.
Awesome prof.  Her class is amazing.
Awesome and super nice. Knows her stuff.
What she taught was inspiring. She could largely raise students' interest in the subject and her class was my best class in my life so far. Her selected texts are excellent.
Denise is my favorite!  I feel like I have learned more from being in one of her classes then I did in four years of University combined.  Look out for her dry sense of humour-hysterical!!!
she inspired me to think that going to grad school would be cool and interesting
If there is a better art history professor out there let me know cause she is the best!!!!
She's compassionate and committed.  Sometimes lets time consuming students take over a bit but she knows her stuff and one learns a lot from her.
DITTO WITH LAST COMMENTS!!!
this woman is so unbelievably cool. I can only hope I'll turn out like her.

AMAZING, SUPPORTIVE, HELPFUL, CLEAR, ENTHUSIASTIC, HONEST, SYMPATHETIC AND TOTALLY INTRIGUING - KEEPS YOU ON YOUR TOES, WITH HUMOUR AND LIGHT-HEARTEDNESS, AND UNDENIABLE PASSION FOR CONTEMPORARY ART AND CULTURE! BEST PROF. IN ART AND CULTURE IN CANADA! I LOVE HER! SHE PARTIES LIKE A ROCKSTAR!
A bit quirky alright but very passionate and smart!
a little quirky - but like a fine wine, just keeps getting better. her personality comes out more in the upper division classes.
Opened my eyes a lot
No Comments
Enthusiastic, committed, caring, knowledgeable, great!
